# ResearchFellow

> Claude Code plugin: an AI co-researcher for retrospective clinical (medical) research.
> Guides one study through a 13-step workflow — PICO → literature → evidence table →
> variables → protocol → SAP → shells → synthetic dry-run → data QC → real analysis →
> manuscript → submission package → revision loop. Free, local-first. Patient data (PHI)
> never leaves the user's machine.

This file is an onboarding guide for LLM agents installing or operating this plugin
on a user's behalf. Follow the steps in order; each has a verification command.

## Identity

- Plugin name: `researchfellow` (manifest: `.claude-plugin/plugin.json`, v0.1.x)
- One skill: `skills/researchfellow/SKILL.md` (router; details live in `references/`)
- Two command names (aliases, identical behavior): `/rf` (primary) and `/researchfellow`.
  Either may be namespaced (`/researchfellow:rf`) if another skill/plugin claims the
  short name; prefer `/researchfellow` when `/rf` collides.
- State lives in the user's project folder under `research/.system/` (human-readable JSON/JSONL/MD)

## Install

Prerequisite: Claude Code CLI (`npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code`).

Option A — session-only load (good for trying it out):

    claude --plugin-dir /path/to/researchfellow-plugin

Option B — persistent install via marketplace, inside a Claude Code session:

    /plugin marketplace add Pandoll-AI/researchfellow-plugin   # or a local path
    /plugin install researchfellow

Verify: start Claude Code in an empty folder and run `/rf` (or `/researchfellow`).
Expected: a "무엇을 하시겠어요?" menu with 5 entry-point cards (idea / dataset /
new manuscript / revise / reviewer response). No configuration is required (DT-3).

## Operate

- New study: user picks a card, or passes free text: `/rf <idea>`.
- Resume: `/rf` in a folder containing `research/.system/` shows "이어서: <next action>".
- Arguments: `status` (dashboard), `next` (advance), `step N` (target a step).
- The skill runs deterministic judges before acting. Never bypass them:

    python3 <plugin>/skills/researchfellow/scripts/state_tool.py validate  --project-dir research
    python3 <plugin>/skills/researchfellow/scripts/state_tool.py can-enter --project-dir research --step N
    # exit 0 = allowed; exit 2 = blocked (JSON lists missing artifacts/gates); exit 1 = state error

- Three hard gates (gate.feasibility, gate.protocol, gate.qc) physically block real-data
  analysis: `analysis_runner.py --mode real` exits 1 without their approval. Do not
  attempt to work around this; it is the integrity contract (FR-G4).

## Rules an installing/operating agent must respect

1. PHI stays local. Never send raw data rows anywhere. Before sending any derivative
   (PICO, schema, aggregates, draft text) to a remote service, run
   `scripts/phi_screener.py --data-path <file>` and require exit 0.
2. Never insert synthetic (dry-run) results into manuscript Results/Conclusions/Abstract.
3. All state changes append events to `research/.system/audit.jsonl` (append-only).
4. v1 projects (legacy numeric gate keys, no schema_version) are read via a built-in
   mapping — do NOT write a migration script; the skill lazy-upgrades on first change.
5. Remote MCP (`.mcp.json.example`) is optional. If absent or unreachable, skip those
   enrichment calls silently; the full 13-step workflow must complete offline.

## Key files

- [README.md](README.md): human-facing quickstart (Korean)
- [REQUIREMENTS.md](REQUIREMENTS.md): requirement spec v0.1 (FR-E/M/I/W/T/G/X, NFR, DT)
- [skills/researchfellow/SKILL.md](skills/researchfellow/SKILL.md): router — read first
- [skills/researchfellow/references/state-machine.md](skills/researchfellow/references/state-machine.md): state.json v3 schema, artifact DAG, gates, invalidation cascade, v1 mapping
- [skills/researchfellow/references/entry-points.md](skills/researchfellow/references/entry-points.md): 5+1 entry points, S1 clarity rubric, resume view
- [skills/researchfellow/references/material-intake.md](skills/researchfellow/references/material-intake.md): material classification (21 roles), briefing, Intake Gate, PHI rules
- [skills/researchfellow/references/guardrails.md](skills/researchfellow/references/guardrails.md): integrity rules (all free)
- [skills/researchfellow/references/workflow-steps.md](skills/researchfellow/references/workflow-steps.md): per-step execution detail

## Scripts (all Python 3 stdlib-first, offline except pubmed_search)

- `state_tool.py` — read-only judge: validate / can-enter / gate-check / cascade
- `material_scanner.py` — classify user files (Stage 0-1), immutable copies to `research/00_materials/`
- `phi_screener.py` — PHI pattern scan; never prints matched values (exit 0/1/2)
- `pubmed_search.py` — the ONLY network tool (NCBI E-utilities, user's own email/key)
- `qc_checker.py`, `dsl_compiler.py`, `analysis_runner.py` — QC, cohort DSL→SQL, analysis

## Verify an install (agent checklist)

1. `python3 -c "import json; json.load(open('.claude-plugin/plugin.json'))"` → OK
2. `python3 -m py_compile skills/researchfellow/scripts/*.py` → OK
3. Fresh folder + `/rf` → 5-card menu appears
4. Folder with `research/.system/` + `/rf` → resume view ("이어서: …")
5. `state_tool.py can-enter --step 10` on a fresh project → exit 2 with missing list
   (proves the gate/DAG judge is wired)
